Caption: Fitting of proposed heat transfer correlation for ultrasound-assisted cooling-heating of cube, sphere or cylinder copper solids immersed in water. The 30 and 100% US settings correspond to irradiation intensities of 4611 and 6914 W/m2, respectively. Process conditions: 5 to 60 °C; 15.2 ≤ Re ≤ 215.2; 4.3 ≤ Pr ≤ 8.5.

Prediction capabilities of heat transfer correlations for ultrasound-assisted cooling of sphere or cube copper solids immersed in a fluid phase formed by ethylene glycol and water (1:1 V/V). Sphere: small symbols; cube: big symbols. The 100% US setting corresponds to an irradiation intensity of 4900 W/m2. Process conditions: 0 to 5 °C; 9.1 ≤ Re ≤ 18.4; 23.5 ≤ Pr ≤ 34.5.

Prediction capabilities of heat transfer correlations for ultrasound-assisted cooling of sphere or cylinder copper solids immersed in a CaCl2 aqueous solution (30 g/L). Sphere: Big symbols; cylinder: small symbols. The 30 and 100% US settings correspond to irradiation intensities of 600 and of 2350 W/m2, respectively. Process conditions: −5 to 5 °C; 12.9 ≤ Re ≤ 29.9; 11.2 ≤ Pr ≤ 18.8.

Experimental (dots) and modeled (lines) thermal history during US-assisted heating of dry-cured ham slices. Simulations conducted by assuming a negligible US power absorbed by the solid, 1-f = 0. Case 1: h from Nu correlation, Eq. (45). Case 2: Negligible external resistance to heat transfer (h → ∞, Bi → ∞). Fitted model corresponds to the estimation of the US source term (1-f) with h from Nu correlation, Eq. (45).
